arts.ttl: Brentford 4-2 Brighton & Hove Albion: Highlights, man of the match, stats as Mbeumo and Wissa shine in six-goal clash
arts.sum: Thomas Frank's Brentford impressed against Brighton & Hove Albion on Saturday afternoon thanks to impressive displays from star forwards Bryan Mbeumo and Yoane Wissa.

arts.ttl: Mbuemo and Wissa power Brentford to decisive win after Pedro dismissed
arts.sum: Brighton’s European dream seems further away than ever

arts.ttl: Mbeumo at the double as Brentford see off 10-man Brighton in six-goal thriller
arts.athr: John Brewin at the Gtech Community Stadium
arts.sum: If both Brentford and Brighton currently appear part of the Premier League furniture, being established in the top tier is a contract written in disappearing ink. Their owners, Matthew Benham and Tony Bloom, estranged former associates, have cracked the alchemy of the transfer market, and are much admired for doing so. Taking the next step forward is a yet more difficult discipline, proving much harder for both. In the battle between teams kicking off in 10th and 11th, Brentford were more progressive, dangerous and calm, only to weather a late storm from an opponent down to 10 men to win 4-2. Christian Nørgaard’s injury-time header from Mathias Jensen’s cross completed victory in a chaotic game, closed out with over 20 minutes of added time after a late collision between Yunus Konak and Jan Paul van Hecke. Brighton’s Dutch defender was carried off while being treated with oxygen and in a neck brace. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: SIX goals, Mbeumo double and a red card! Brentford beat Brighton

arts.ttl: Bryan Mbeumo at the double as Brentford beat Brighton to stay in European hunt

arts.ttl: Premier League give ruling on Joao Pedro's first red card for 'violent conduct' against Brentford
